# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

All publicly released versions of this project are considered supported.

However, fixes and improvements are applied to the latest codebase.  
Users may be asked to upgrade to the latest version when reporting issues.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

If you discover a security issue in this project, **do not open a public issue**.

Instead, use GitHub’s [Private Vulnerability Reporting](https://github.com/pS3ud0RAnD0m/attackframework-burp-exporter/security/advisories/new).

This ensures the issue is reviewed without public exposure.

For non-security bugs or feature requests, use the issue forms. For setup questions or broader
discussion, use GitHub Discussions.

## What to Include

Please include as much detail as possible:

- Affected version or commit
- Clear description of the issue and impact
- Steps to reproduce
- Proof of concept, if safe to share
- Relevant logs, screenshots, requests, responses, or payloads

## Scope

This policy applies to vulnerabilities in:

- The Burp extension code
- Data handling and export logic
- Integration logic with external systems (e.g., OpenSearch)

This policy does **not** cover:

- Vulnerabilities in third-party tools or dependencies themselves
- Issues in target applications tested using this tool
- Local environment, deployment, or operational misconfiguration not caused by this project

## Disclosure

Please allow time for the issue to be reviewed and addressed before any public disclosure.

Given the nature of this project, fixes are handled on a **best-effort basis**.  
Response and remediation timelines are not guaranteed.

## Safe Harbor / Legal

If you make a good-faith effort to follow this policy when reporting a vulnerability, we will not intentionally pursue or support legal action against you.

To qualify, please:

- Use GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting
- Avoid public disclosure until the issue has been reviewed and addressed, or disclosure is agreed upon
- Avoid actions that would degrade, disrupt, or destroy systems or data
- Avoid accessing or retaining data beyond what is necessary to demonstrate the issue
- Stop testing and report promptly if sensitive data is encountered

This safe harbor applies only to this repository and its code.  
It does not apply to third-party systems or environments without permission.

## Notes

This is an open-source, offensive security-focused tool intended for research and testing.

Security reports are appreciated, but this is an early-stage project.  
Support, fixes, and follow-up are provided on a best-effort basis.
